question.text: As per SEBI's 2020 circular, mutual fund schemes that are being wound up must be:

answer.text: Listed on stock exchanges to provide exit route to investors during the interim period
Merged with another scheme of the same AMC
Kept in suspended mode until market conditions improve

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI's May 2020 circular requires wound-up schemes to be listed on stock exchanges, providing an exit route to investors during the potentially long period before all assets are liquidated. This is an investor protection measure. **The Trap:** Winding up doesn't mean instant liquidation — it can take time to realize all assets, and listing provides interim liquidity. **Cross-Exam Context:** This SEBI circular (post-Franklin Templeton episode) is tested in V-A and X-A for regulatory awareness. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If a client's fund is being wound up, advise them to check the secondary market price vs NAV — in stressed situations, units may trade at a significant discount to NAV, and patience may yield better realization.
